prompt: An indicator LED is driven straight from a 5 V GPIO pin. It flashes bright, then goes dark for good. What is missing?
snippet:
5V GPIO ───▶|─── GND
            (LED)

   no series resistor in the path
choices: The GPIO cannot source enough current
A flyback diode across the LED
reveal: With no series resistor, nothing limits the current except the LED and source impedance — well past the LED rating. It overheats and fails (often after one bright flash).
lesson: Every LED on a voltage source needs current limiting. Red LED (Vf ≈ 2 V) at 10 mA on 5 V: R = (5 − 2) / 0.01 = 300 Ω → use 330 Ω (E12).
caption: One bright flash, then dead. The fix is one part.
